title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: dracut bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for dracut is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The dracut packages contain an event-driven initial RAM file system (initramfs) generator infrastructure based on the udev device manager. The virtual file system, initramfs, is loaded together with the kernel at boot time and initializes the system, so it can read and boot from the root partition.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): dracut needs to support populating /etc/multipath/wwids from the kernel commandline [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-109565)  rd.multipath default for user_friendly_names is enable and in coreos may cause timouts during boot [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-96101)
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64 - 4 years of updates
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems - 4 years of updates
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 - Update Services for SAP Solutions 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 - Extended Update Support 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server for Power LE - Update Services for SAP Solutions
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems - Extended Update Support
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64 - Extended Update Support
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian - Extended Update Support
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server - AUS
full_text:
An update for dracut is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The dracut packages contain an event-driven initial RAM file system (initramfs) generator infrastructure based on the udev device manager. The virtual file system, initramfs, is loaded together with the kernel at boot time and initializes the system, so it can read and boot from the root partition.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): dracut needs to support populating /etc/multipath/wwids from the kernel commandline [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-109565)  rd.multipath default for user_friendly_names is enable and in coreos may cause timouts during boot [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-96101)
